title: Handle machine start state when sent a signal
body:
In the instance where the user sends a signal, such as SIGINT (Ctl-c), when a Podman Machine is in the middle of starting, make sure the state doesn't get stuck in the "Currently Starting" status.

Resolves: #24416

```release-note
Fix a bug where a Podman Machine would have the incorrect state if sent SIGINT
```

comments.body: I don't have a Windows machine accessible to me at the moment, but I was able to confirm this works on Linux/macOS by doing a start and using Ctl-c to send a SIGINT

> btw one thing I have not looked at what happens to the child processes we start gvproxy/vfkit/krunkit/qemu... Are they correctly killed as well?

I've done a couple tests by trying to wait various amounts of times after seeing the "Starting machine <name>" message and sending SIGINT. There doesn't seem to be any issues with the helper binaries still running.

I'm not sure if there's a better way to test that sort of thing
LGTM if we're confident the helper binaries are being killed properly
